title: viola arvensis subsp. bellioides
intro: Viola arvensis subsp. bellioides, also known as woodland violet, is a subspecies of the common field pansy that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Viola arvensis subsp. bellioides include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Viola arvensis subsp. bellioides is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
